prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to design a scheduling dashboard for a recording studio. The operator must resolve today's room, crew, gear, and approval conflicts faster than they can in a spreadsheet.
must: identify the operator's first decision
reject a generic KPI-card dashboard
derive one product-specific direction from scheduling conflicts
cover one failure or recovery state
define desktop and narrow proof
must_not: lead with decorative metrics
invent customer proof
claim visual quality without rendered evidence

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to create a landing page for a small architectural lighting studio. It needs to feel specific, physical, and expensive without default beige luxury styling.
must: make the fixture or lighting result the first-view artifact
derive palette and composition from a physical source
handle the real-asset gap honestly
place proof before decorative narrative
check the mobile first viewport
must_not: replace product imagery with div art
use a centered hero plus three equal feature cards
use fictional metrics or logos as proof

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to reimagine an existing media editor whose canvas is buried under status panels. Keep routes, shortcuts, saved-project behavior, and accessibility wins intact.
must: name the accidental visual priority
state what must be killed and what must survive
make the canvas dominate the new composition
preserve the explicit product contracts
compare the result with the baseline
must_not: rewrite unrelated architecture
hide diagnostics without a recovery path
stop at advice when implementation is requested and editable

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to roast this implementation and screenshot. I want the real design crime, the source cause, and the redesign—not polite feedback.
must: attack the artifact rather than the author
open with the dominant design failure
connect every major blow to evidence and user harm
prescribe exact cuts or replacements
end with a sharp verdict
must_not: use a praise sandwich
substitute jokes for evidence
say improve hierarchy or spacing without naming elements

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to certify that this new interface is production-ready. Only source code is available; there is no runnable URL or screenshot.
must: refuse a verified visual-quality claim
run or request the smallest available static evidence
state runtime and visual proof as blocked
name the exact remaining claim limit
must_not: return excellent from a clean static scan
treat missing evidence as a pass
claim production-ready

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to redesign a hospital-capacity dashboard so an operations lead can decide where to move staff. Forecasts include uncertainty, missing feeds, and zero-capacity wards.
must: start from the staffing decision rather than chart variety
distinguish zero, missing, stale, and uncertain data
choose encodings and scales that support comparison
provide an accessible table or equivalent exact values
annotate the consequence or threshold that changes the decision
must_not: use decorative KPI cards as the primary answer
encode critical meaning with color alone
invent certainty or interpolate missing data silently

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to create a daily practice interface for an experimental electronic musician. It is a repeated-use product, but it should feel like an instrument rather than neutral enterprise software.
must: calibrate expression, density, motion, familiarity, trust, and emotional tone from the task
keep repeated controls learnable and fast
derive visual character from sound, performance, or the musical object
use motion as feedback or continuity rather than ambient theater
prove the core practice loop and a narrow layout
must_not: default to neutrals plus one restrained accent solely because it is a product surface
turn every control into bespoke art
hide latency or state behind animation

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to create a site for a restored silent-film season. The experience should build curiosity and atmosphere before revealing the full programme; it is cultural, not a SaaS funnel.
must: justify delayed disclosure as part of the audience experience
derive art direction from real film or archive material
preserve orientation and an accessible route to the programme
calibrate pacing and motion instead of forcing immediate conversion
state asset rights or availability gaps honestly
must_not: force a dominant signup CTA into the first viewport
apply a stock proof-wall or pricing-funnel structure
use mystery to hide basic navigation or essential access

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to tear apart this overloaded incident console, but do not destroy its excellent keyboard-driven event scrubber while redesigning the shell.
must: name the dominant systemic failure
identify the scrubber as an explicit preservation constraint
use no more cuts than real root causes justify
attach evidence, damage, cause, exact fix, and an earned roast to major causes
state how the redesign keeps the preserved mechanism visible and usable
must_not: pad the critique to exactly five findings
praise-sandwich the verdict
replace the scrubber merely to make the redesign look more original

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to implement a production-ready multilingual account-settings surface rendered on the server. Saving should feel immediate, but the network can be slow and the same account may be edited in another tab.
must: treat server validation and authorization as final
define optimistic pending, reconciliation, rejection rollback, and retry behavior
prevent stale responses from overwriting newer state
define an explicit cross-tab conflict or synchronization contract
keep locale, time zone, identifiers, and initial values deterministic across SSR hydration
must_not: leave rejected optimistic values presented as saved
assume arrival order or last-write-wins is correct
format server and client values with implicit machine locale

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to harden a production mobile article whose navigation drawer overlays image-led content on notched phones. The current drawer leaks background scroll, loses focus on close, and delays the lead image.
must: keep controls clear of safe-area insets
define modal focus entry, escape, containment, and return
scope touch-action and overscroll containment without disabling zoom or browser gestures globally
reserve responsive media dimensions and prioritize the decision-critical lead image
provide meaningful image failure and narrow-layout behavior
must_not: disable user scaling
return focus to body after the drawer closes
preload the whole image gallery or lazy-load the lead image

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to implement a playable WebGL material prototype with an export action. It must survive unsupported graphics, reduced motion, tab/background transitions, and export failure without producing a blank decorative shell.
must: make the playable artifact visible and nonblank before calling the prototype successful
feature-detect WebGL and provide an honest static or reduced capability fallback
preserve product identity and task clarity under reduced motion
pause or throttle offscreen work and dispose graphics resources on teardown
preserve the artifact and provide recovery when export fails
must_not: treat successful canvas creation as proof of visible output
burn render cycles while offscreen without product need
hide export failure in a disappearing toast

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to broadly redesign and implement a motion-heavy media inspector whose core workflow includes a toolbar with neighboring tooltips, an animated SVG mode glyph, and a shared-layout selection indicator that moves between tools.
must: delay the first pointer tooltip but sharply reduce delay between neighboring triggers while keeping keyboard disclosure immediate
verify the SVG bounding box, transform-box, transform-origin, and whether a wrapper group is required
give the shared-layout selection a unique stable identity and coordinate entry and exit order
animate between compatible values and prevent false replay of saved state with initial false where appropriate
test the shipped production path and reduced-motion result, profiling representative hardware when complexity or evidence makes performance a risk
must_not: force keyboard users through the pointer tooltip delay
reuse one layout identity for unrelated objects
treat development-mode stutter or one flagship machine as performance proof

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to redesign a product editor from supplied screenshots and a short interaction video. Extract the underlying system without cloning its visual costume.
must: record each decisive source and its role
preserve viewport, route or state, theme, timestamp or video frame, and crop boundaries
name the extracted system, constraints, what not to copy, and proof gaps
convert observations into tokens, components, layout rules, and proof targets
compare the implemented result against reproducible reference evidence
must_not: treat an unlabelled crop as reproducible evidence
copy the reference costume without adapting its system to the product task
invent missing source context or provenance

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to finish this implemented desktop editor. The concept is strong, but repeated rows drift by a few pixels, gaps are inconsistent, native scrollbars remain, gradients feel accidental, and several hand-drawn SVG icons look cheap. Fix the real causes and prove the final interface from browser captures.
must: run separate structure and finish passes
measure horizontal and vertical anchors plus repeated spacing relationships
customize every remaining scroll region without hiding its affordance
judge gradients by rendered role and execution rather than banning them
replace or deliberately rebuild weak custom vector icons within a coherent family
inspect device-scale-factor-2 captures and focused detail crops
record a finish ledger and continue while any applicable dimension fails
must_not: approve the result from source or a clean detector alone
use a low-resolution full-page thumbnail as finish proof
stop after local nudges leave the same visible defect
ban all gradients or hide scrollbars to avoid styling them

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to redesign a browser-based color-grading studio used all day by expert editors. The preview, timeline, scopes, inspectors, autosave, undo history, and export flow already exist, but the current shell treats them like a generic analytics dashboard.
must: classify the product as a creative studio rather than a dashboard
make the current frame timeline and selected object the sovereign artifact
organize inspector controls by editing intent and selection scope
preserve undo autosave conflict and export contracts
prove selected unsaved conflicting collapsed-panel and export-failure states
must_not: lead with KPIs or render metrics
apply marketing-scale typography to repeated controls
treat one static canvas screenshot as proof of the editing loop

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to redesign a regional fleet command center where operators monitor continuously, acknowledge incidents, assign owners, inspect stale feeds, coordinate handoff, and recover failed actions.
must: distinguish a command center from a passive dashboard
model normal degraded incident acknowledged assigned recovering and resolved states
keep freshness ownership evidence and next safe action connected
reserve high-salience color and motion for response-worthy change
prove alert flood stale source partial failure handoff and recovery
must_not: use sci-fi glow or fake terminal chrome as operational identity
make every panel equally urgent
hide stale or failed subsystems behind an aggregate green status

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to redesign the existing HTML/CSS HUD and menus around a cooperative browser game. Do not change its renderer or gameplay systems. The interface must work in calm exploration, noisy combat, controller and keyboard modes, ultrawide and narrow viewports, and long localization.
must: classify live HUD pause inventory and settings as distinct game moments
protect the gameplay focal area and assign persistent information to safe zones
tie salience to urgency objective and player intent rather than card size
verify input glyph switching controller focus localization and representative scene contrast
route renderer and gameplay-system implementation outside the web UI scope
must_not: apply an admin dashboard composition to live play
validate readability only on a flat or calm background
put scrolling panels or native app sidebars over the gameplay viewport

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to create a public launch page for a level-design studio with a real interactive editor preview and an embedded play-test state. Keep persuasion, editor controls, and game HUD behavior coherent without averaging them into one visual register.
must: declare landing studio and game regions with one primary context per moment
apply brand rules to persuasion product rules to editor controls and game rules to play-test HUD
choose composition and scrolling behavior separately for each boundary
preserve a real artifact as proof instead of forging a fake editor
prove the public first viewport editor interaction and representative play-test state
must_not: use one generic dashboard shell across all regions
let cinematic landing motion infect high-frequency editor controls
classify the whole product from its dark visual costume

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to redesign a motion-heavy incident map. Nodes move between regions, a scene cover masks mode changes, alerts update live, and operators can drag, reverse, cancel, or invoke reduced motion.
must: classify events as feedback state spatial attention or ambient motion
persist a motion map with purpose frequency origin destination interruption reduced mode and proof
define one bounded duration easing displacement and attention grammar
commit mode changes at an observable covered milestone instead of a guessed timeout
preserve identity and retarget interruption from the current rendered value
test repeated triggers rapid reversal reduced motion offscreen behavior and cleanup
must_not: apply one entrance preset to every element
hide primary content until JavaScript reveals it
use constant pulsing or ambient movement as proof of operational activity
remount components merely to restart animation

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to review this command-center screenshot and implementation, propose the redesign, and deliver a beautiful portable HTML dossier that another designer can continue without this chat.
must: persist report-manifest.json and standalone report.html
embed local screenshots and use normalized numbered annotations with a matching text legend
include verdict context evidence systemic findings exact moves do-not-break proof risks and limitations
escape hostile content and expose missing or corrupt evidence visibly
open the report at desktop and narrow widths and inspect its print and focus behavior
keep automated detector findings labeled as leads rather than human visual verdicts
must_not: depend on external CSS JavaScript or remote images
treat the HTML dossier itself as proof that the proposed design works
silently omit a broken screenshot
duplicate the entire report into the chat closeout

prompt: Use $ruthless-designer to create a distinctive logistics control room without the cheap generated look of equal cards, fake terminal chrome, decorative activity, and invented metrics.
must: derive major decisions from the real workflow data pressure audience or spatial model
identify what a competitor could not reuse after a noun swap
use a component ecology instead of one repeated card container
label real synthetic missing and blocked evidence honestly
make every interactive-looking control functional or explicitly noninteractive
run the cheap-generation gate across content rhythm motion signature and costly states
must_not: replace generic SaaS styling with automatic brutalism beige luxury or sci-fi chrome
invent customers metrics logs or proof
defend distinction with only fonts colors gradients or effects
